air Political ractices Commissio11., 
••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
August 19, 1976 
1vlr. S. L. Burr 
Staff Assistant 
Office of City Manager 
1500 Warburton Avenue 
Santa Clara, California 
Dear Mr. Burr: 
I have been asked to respond to your letter of July 27, 1976, 
addressed to Chairman Lowenstein. 
You asked the following 
question: 
W, are contemplating having designated persons for 
agencies of the City file statements of economic 
i~terest pursuant to a Conflict of Interest Code with 
the Office of the City Clerk rather than with the 
Agency. 
Please inform us whether the Political Reform Act of 
74 as 
by SB 1373 would preclude us from 
adopting this policy. 
Senate Bill 1373, amending Government Code Section 81005(g), 
provides: 
All reports and statements required by Chapter 7 -
one 
original with the agencYi provided that for reports 
and statements filed by the head of the agency, a 
member of a board or commission not under a department 
of state government or not under the jurisdiction of a 
local legislative body, and ~ person who files a 
financial disclosure report pursuant to Article 2 
(commencing with Section 87200) of Chapter 7, the 
agency shall make and retain a copy and fODvard the 
original to the code reviewing body. 
e Pol 
1 Reform Act of 1974, as amended 
SB 1373, 
would permit adoption of 
contemplated procedure under 
the 
llowing conditions: 
1. 
As §81005(g) 
0 
the Act 
to 
th the 
6 
such statements 
ting in 
:MENT CONFLiCT 
iNTFREST 322 


. , 
8-19-76 
this arrangement will have to delegate its authority for 
receipt and administration of such statements to the city 
clerk. 
It must be clear, however, that the agency will 
retain responsibility for receipt of statements and 
administration of the duties of the filing officer, pur-
suant to the provisions of §82027 of the Act. 
The 
following sentence should be sufficient to accomplish these 
purpose: 
The 
herewith delegates authority 
(Name of agency) 
for receipt of statements of economic interests and admin-
istration of the duties of the filing officer, pursuant 
to Government Code §§81010 and 91013 to the Office of the 
City Clerk, provided, however, that the responsibility for 
timely receipt of such statements and the proper 
administration of such statements, remains with the 
(Name of agency) 
2. 
To insure compliance with §81008 of the Act, relating 
to public records, the city clerk must retain the original 
statement and forward a copy to 
e agency. 
The agency and 
the clerk must make the statements accessible to the public, 
pursuant to the provisions of Government Code SalODS, which 
provides: 
Every report and statement filed pursuant to this title 
is a public record open for public inspection and 
reproduction dur 
regular business hours, commencing 
as soon as practicable, but in any event not later than 
the second business day following the day on which it 
was received. 
No conditions whatsoever shall be imposed 
upon persons desiring to inspect or reproduce reports 
and statements filed under this title, nor shall any 
information or identification be required from such 
persons. 
Copies shall be provided at a charge not to 
exceed ten cents ($0.10) per page. 
Campaign statements 
shall be open for public inspection and reproduction 
from 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. on the Saturday and Sunday 
preceding a statewide election. 
I trust this has answered your question. 
If we can be of 
further assistance, please do not hesitate to call us at 916/ 
322-6444. 
Sincerely, 
DLS: jhm 
Spurlock, Jr" 
nterest Divis

Very truly yours.

74 as 
by SB 1373 would preclude us from 
adopting this policy. 
Senate Bill 1373, amending Government Code Section 81005(g), 
provides: 
All reports and statements required by Chapter 7 -

The

istration of the duties of the filing officer, pursuant 
to Government Code §§81010 and 91013 to the Office of the 
City Clerk, provided, however, that the responsibility for

sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act of 1974, as amended by SB 1373, would permit adoption of the contemplated procedure under the following conditions: 1. The agency will have to delegate its authority for receipt and administration of such statements to the city clerk, with the agency retaining ultimate responsibility for timely receipt and proper administration. 2. The city clerk must retain the original statement and forward a copy to the agency, with both the agency and clerk making statements accessible to the public pursuant to Government Code §81008.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Political Reform Act of 1974, as amended by SB 1373, permits a city to have designated persons file statements of economic interest with the City Clerk's office rather than directly with their respective agencies.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that cities may file statements of economic interest with the City Clerk instead of the agency if the agency properly delegates this authority while retaining ultimate responsibility, and if the clerk retains originals while forwarding copies to the agency and ensuring public accessibility of all records.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated July 27, 1976) and the FPPC response letter (dated August 19, 1976). The response clearly identifies the question, cites applicable statute (SB 1373 amending Gov. Code §81005(g)), and provides detailed conclusions with specific conditions. Minor OCR degradation in some portions but primary content is clear.

i~terest pursuant to a Conflict of Interest Code with 
the Office of the City Clerk rather than with the

74 as

The

The 
herewith delegates authority 
(Name of agency) 
for receipt of statements of economic interests and admin-
istration of the duties of the filing officer, pursuant 
to Government Code §§81010 and 91013 to the Office of the 
City Clerk, provided, however, that the responsibility for

2. 
To insure compliance with §81008 of the Act, relating

The agency and

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act of 1974, as amended by SB 1373, would permit adoption of the contemplated procedure under the following conditions: 1. The agency will have to delegate its authority for receipt and administration of such statements to the city clerk, with the agency retaining ultimate responsibility for timely receipt and proper administration. 2. The city clerk must retain the original statement and forward a copy to the agency, with both the agency and clerk making statements accessible to the public pursuant to Government Code §81008.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter responding to Santa Clara's inquiry about whether they can have designated city employees file statements of economic interest with the City Clerk rather than their respective agencies under the Political Reform Act of 1974 as amended by SB 1373. The FPPC approved the arrangement subject to specific conditions regarding delegation of authority and public record requirements.
